case.why_selected: Tests whether the schema can hold a corporate and multi-brand design-system case where public evidence emphasizes components, foundations, scalability, cohesion, and preservation of distinct resort identities.

public_case_page.title: Grandvalira Resorts x FLOC* - The Design System for guiding the three ski resorts

public_case_copy_excerpts.text: The Design System for guiding the three ski resorts
create a hybrid Design System that unites the three emblematic resorts of Andorra
Pal Arinsal, Grandvalira and Ordino Arcalis
library of multi-brand components and foundations
allows the scalability and visual cohesion of each resort
respecting the uniqueness of their multiple personalities
reinforcing the digital universe of the main brand

confidence_semantics.high: Directly stated in public copy, directly listed in public metadata, or anchored to existing local visual observations with direct visible evidence.
confidence_semantics.medium: Reasonable interpretation from public copy or from a single anchored visual observation, but not independently repeated.
confidence_semantics.low: Plausible but weak inference from aesthetics, tone, or incomplete evidence; requires human review before reuse.

extracted_facts.fact: The public case frames the project as a design system for guiding three ski resorts.
The public copy names the three resorts as Pal Arinsal, Grandvalira, and Ordino Arcalis.
The public copy describes the work as a hybrid Design System that unites the three resort identities.
The public copy says FLOC* created a library of multi-brand components and foundations.
The public page identifies UX/UI and Design System as service areas.

visual_observations.observation: The public description anchors the case in system behavior: a hybrid design system, multi-brand components, and foundations.
visual_observations.downstream_note: Treat as stated system architecture until captures or component documentation confirm how the system appears.

visual_observations.observation: The case states a need for visual cohesion across each resort while preserving their unique personalities.
visual_observations.downstream_note: This is a strong design-system claim, but the record does not yet verify visible differentiation between resort identities.

visual_observations.observation: The public copy implies a parent-brand hierarchy: the system supports resort-level cohesion while reinforcing the main brand's digital universe.
visual_observations.downstream_note: Parent-child hierarchy is inferred from case language and needs component or page evidence before reuse.

visual_observations.observation: The public description presents scalability as a visible-system property of the component and foundation library.
visual_observations.downstream_note: Scalability is stated directly, but actual scalability evidence would require multiple screens, components, or variants.

strategic_interpretations.interpretation: Grandvalira appears to use design-system work as a governance layer for multiple resort identities rather than as a single-brand refresh.
strategic_interpretations.why_not_high_confidence: The governance role is a Brand3 interpretation of design-system language; source copy does not use governance terminology.

strategic_interpretations.interpretation: The case suggests a tension between unity and differentiation: the system must make the resorts feel related without flattening their individual identities.
strategic_interpretations.why_not_high_confidence: The tension is strongly suggested by copy, but visible execution across the three resorts is not yet captured.

strategic_interpretations.interpretation: The project frames digital product coherence as an ecosystem problem across multiple destinations, not as isolated UX/UI screens.
strategic_interpretations.why_not_high_confidence: The ecosystem reading depends on Brand3 interpretation of 'digital universe' and UX/UI service scope.

unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.inference: The visual system may use shared foundations with resort-specific tokens, themes, or component variants.
unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.why_low_confidence: The copy names components, foundations, and personalities, but does not specify tokens, themes, or variants.

unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.inference: The design language may balance institutional utility with destination-specific atmosphere.
unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.why_low_confidence: Atmospheric tone is inferred from 'The infinite winter' and resort context, not visually confirmed.

unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.inference: The system may prioritize navigational consistency across resort experiences.
unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.why_low_confidence: The word 'guiding' and UX/UI service tag imply navigation, but no flow or interface capture is included.

contradictions.stated_claim: The public copy says the hybrid design system unites three resorts.
contradictions.observed_signal: The same public copy says the system respects the uniqueness of their multiple personalities.
contradictions.interpretation_boundary: This is a productive multi-brand design-system tension, not evidence of inconsistency.

contradictions.stated_claim: The public copy claims scalability and visual cohesion for each resort.
contradictions.observed_signal: The available first-pass evidence does not include component screenshots, token documentation, or multi-resort screen comparisons.
contradictions.interpretation_boundary: Do not convert scalability and cohesion claims into confirmed system performance without component or screen evidence.

review_questions: Do captures or design-system materials confirm shared components and foundations across the three resort identities?
Where does the system preserve each resort's unique personality, and where does it enforce main-brand cohesion?
Is the unity/differentiation tension visible in interface behavior or only stated in copy?
Which terms are reusable for Brand3 design-system cases without importing resort-specific language?
